title: World Diving Calendar
description: Year-round diving conditions for global destinations, organized by month. Each destination includes monthly surface water temperature (degC), currents, marine-life highlights, conditions/visibility and a season rating (Peak / Good / Shoulder / Low / Closed).

rating_legend.Peak: Best conditions and/or signature marine life
rating_legend.Closed: Not accessible (park closure or no operators)

destinations.name: Fernando de Noronha
destinations.currents: Generally mild; some sites moderate current and swell
destinations.highlights: Huge spinner dolphin pods with sharks, turtles and 30m+ visibility

Jan.conditions: Warm, rainy season onset, good viz

Dec.conditions: Warm, dry tailing off, good viz

destinations.name: Bahamas
destinations.currents: Mostly moderate; baited shark dives are stationary, some sites exposed to ocean current
destinations.highlights: World's premier shark diving - hammerheads, tigers, and whitetips

Oct.marine_life: Tiger sharks (Tiger Beach building), reef sharks

Nov.conditions: Tiger season strong, cooling, good viz

destinations.name: Bay Islands
destinations.currents: Generally mild across the group; light drift on walls and channels, gentle seamount current
destinations.highlights: Roatán, Utila & Guanaja - affordable Mesoamerican Reef walls plus whale sharks

Jan.conditions: Rainy tail, nortes possible north shores

Feb.conditions: Whale shark season starts, drying out

Apr.conditions: Dry season, flat seas, excellent viz

Dec.conditions: Rainy, cooler nortes on north shores

destinations.name: Bonaire
destinations.currents: Generally mild on leeward west coast; stronger off Klein Bonaire and the wild windward east side
destinations.highlights: World's best shore-diving with healthy fringing reefs, dive-when-you-want freedom

Sep.conditions: Outside hurricane belt but fringe storms possible

Oct.conditions: Warm water, viz returning, short rains

destinations.name: Cayman Islands
destinations.currents: Mostly mild; can pick up along the dramatic deep walls and at exposed sites
destinations.highlights: Crystal walls, Stingray City, and Little Cayman's Bloody Bay Wall

Feb.conditions: Nassau grouper aggregate around full moon

Jun.conditions: Warm, calm, start of wet season

Jul.conditions: Warm water, generally calm, viz high

destinations.name: Cozumel
destinations.currents: Famous for drift diving; currents can be strong, especially southern reefs like Palancar and Punta Sur
destinations.highlights: Effortless high-viz drift dives along vibrant Mesoamerican reef walls

Jun.conditions: Warm, high viz, early wet season

destinations.name: Great Blue Hole
destinations.currents: Sheltered and calm inside the sinkhole; surrounding Lighthouse Reef can have mild current
destinations.best_months: Apr-Jun (dry, calm); Mar-Jun for Gladden Spit whale sharks
destinations.highlights: Iconic deep blue sinkhole with stalactites and circling reef sharks

Jan.conditions: Dry season, calm, viz 30m+, occasional fronts

Feb.marine_life: Reef sharks, nurse sharks, turtles nearby

Mar.marine_life: Reef sharks; whale sharks at Gladden Spit
Mar.conditions: Dry, flat, top viz, whale shark season begins

Apr.marine_life: Reef sharks; Gladden Spit whale sharks

Jun.marine_life: Reef sharks; late whale sharks at Gladden
Jun.conditions: Wet season begins, still good viz

Jul.marine_life: Reef sharks, nurse sharks, reef fish

Sep.conditions: Peak hurricane season, trips often canceled

Oct.conditions: Hurricane risk, wettest month, rough crossings

Nov.conditions: Wet easing, seas settling, viz recovering

Dec.conditions: Dry season returns, calm, good viz

destinations.name: Roatán
destinations.currents: Generally mild and beginner-friendly; some channels and walls have light drift
destinations.highlights: Affordable wall and reef diving on the Mesoamerican Reef, great for beginners

Jan.conditions: Rainy season tail, possible nortes north shore

Apr.conditions: Dry season, flat seas, excellent viz

Dec.conditions: Rainy, cooler nortes on north shore

destinations.name: Utila
destinations.currents: Mostly mild and gentle; some seamounts and north-side sites have light current
destinations.highlights: Budget dive-cert hub with year-round whale shark chances

Jan.conditions: Rainy tail, possible nortes, viz variable

Mar.conditions: Prime whale shark window, calm, dry

Apr.conditions: Whale shark season, flat seas, top viz

Jun.conditions: Warm, generally calm, early wet season

Sep.conditions: Whale shark season, fringe storm risk

destinations.name: Sipadan Island
destinations.currents: Currents are the key variable (stronger=more action); Barracuda Point (barracuda tornado, dawn hammerheads), South Point (strongest, pelagics), Drop Off (gentle)
destinations.highlights: Turtle & barracuda-tornado wall; island closed every November.

Jan.marine_life: Barracuda tornado, jackfish, reef sharks, turtles; CNY window
Jan.conditions: Cooler; N wind can cut viz to 10-20m

Mar.marine_life: Turtles (20+/dive), barracuda, reef sharks; nesting begins

Apr.marine_life: Turtle nesting begins; barracuda tornado, bumpheads

May.marine_life: Turtle nesting full swing; peak conditions

Jun.marine_life: Turtles abundant; barracuda tornado, reef sharks

Aug.conditions: S winds can briefly cut viz; still good

Nov.marine_life: ISLAND CLOSED for conservation (nearby Mabul/Kapalai divable)

Dec.conditions: Rainy onset; viz softer; slightly cooler

destinations.name: Great Barrier Reef
destinations.currents: Inner reefs mild; outer/Ribbon Reef walls drift dives timed to tides; Coral Sea/Osprey significant currents (North Horn shark feed); SS Yongala strong/severe current - slack tide only
destinations.highlights: World's largest reef; dwarf minke whales Jun-Jul; coral spawning Nov.

Jan.marine_life: Green turtle nesting (Raine Is); reef life
Jan.conditions: Mid-summer wet; warmest; stinger season; cyclone risk

Feb.conditions: Late summer wet; warm; stingers; cyclone risk

Apr.conditions: Autumn; wet tapering; stinger season ending

May.marine_life: Reef life; manta plankton blooms begin; minke season starts
May.conditions: Dry begins; trade winds; reef 'as good as it gets'

Jun.marine_life: DWARF MINKE WHALES (Ribbon Reefs); humpbacks; mantas

Jul.marine_life: Dwarf minke whales (peak); humpbacks; potato cod
Jul.conditions: Mid-winter; coldest; best viz; trade winds

Aug.conditions: Often BEST month: dry, calm, clear; Coral Sea viz 40-100m

Sep.conditions: Early spring dry; calm, clear; fewer crowds

Oct.marine_life: Reef sharks; potato cod; humpbacks tail
Oct.conditions: Spring dry; trades easing; calm seas, great viz

Nov.marine_life: CORAL MASS SPAWNING (after full moon); turtle nesting begins
Nov.conditions: Late spring; warming; stinger season starts

Dec.marine_life: Green turtle nesting (Raine Is); coral spawning spill
Dec.conditions: Early summer wet; warm; stingers; cyclone risk

destinations.name: Whitsunday Islands
destinations.currents: Generally mild on reef; outer reef can have moderate tidal currents
destinations.highlights: Heart Reef and Hardy Reef coral gardens with winter whale encounters

Feb.conditions: Peak cyclone season, rain, lower viz

Nov.conditions: Warm, viz good before wet season

Dec.conditions: Warm, wet season onset, viz variable

destinations.name: Heron Island
destinations.region: Coral Sea / Southern Great Barrier Reef
destinations.currents: Mild to moderate tidal currents at channel sites like Heron Bommie
destinations.highlights: Coral cay with shore diving, manta cleaning stations and turtle nesting

destinations.name: Komodo National Park
destinations.currents: Tidal, strongest at full/new moon; powerful drift; Castle Rock/Crystal Rock (reef-hook pinnacles), Batu Bolong (ferocious flank currents), The Cauldron/Shotgun (committed drift); downcurrents at pinnacles. N warm/calm, S cold upwelling/rough
destinations.highlights: Strong tidal drifts; warm north vs cold-upwelling south; mantas year-round by zone.

Jan.marine_life: South mantas PEAK (Manta Alley); hammerheads (south); macro explosion
Jan.conditions: Wet; lower viz (5-15m), choppier north; Dec can be 30m+

Feb.marine_life: South manta PEAK (Jan-Feb); macro (Rhinopias, frogfish); hammerheads
Feb.conditions: Wet; lower viz; southern manta aggregations largest

Mar.marine_life: South mantas; hammerheads (to early Apr); rich macro

Apr.marine_life: N/Central mantas active; whale sharks (Apr-Jun); reef sharks
Apr.conditions: Dry begins; SWEET SPOT; manageable currents

May.marine_life: N/Central mantas; whale sharks; reef sharks
May.conditions: Dry; SINGLE BEST MONTH; clear, calm, fewer crowds

Jun.marine_life: N/Central mantas; whale sharks; reef sharks

Jul.marine_life: N mantas; reef sharks; mola season nears
Jul.conditions: Dry; best N viz; peak cold upwelling south; busy

Aug.marine_life: MOLA MOLA peak (south stations); N mantas; reef sharks
Aug.conditions: Dry; N viz up to 30m; south cold/green; busiest

Sep.marine_life: Mola mola; N/Central mantas; reef sharks
Sep.conditions: Dry; great N viz; upwelling south

Oct.marine_life: Mola tail; N mantas (Karang Makassar); reef sharks

Nov.marine_life: South mantas building (Manta Alley); reef sharks

Dec.marine_life: South manta peak begins; macro; reef sharks; dragons active
Dec.conditions: Wet; lower viz but can be 30m+; choppier north

destinations.name: Raja Ampat
destinations.currents: Drift diving central; strongest at Dampier Strait (Cape Kri, Sardine Reef, Blue Magic) driving nutrient upwellings; plankton/current build from May
destinations.highlights: Most biodiverse reefs on Earth; manta peak Dec-Mar.

Jan.marine_life: Manta peak (Manta Sandy/Ridge); wobbegongs, walking sharks, pygmy seahorses
Jan.conditions: Peak NW monsoon; viz 20-30m+; secondary rain spike

May.marine_life: Macro & night diving shine; mantas scattered

Jul.conditions: Low season: rough 1.5-3m, wettest; liveaboards pause

Aug.conditions: Stormy; low season; many liveaboards reposition

Sep.marine_life: Macro; reef life; mantas returning late

destinations.name: Okinawa Islands
destinations.currents: Strong drift at Yonaguni (hammerheads); Ishigaki Manta Scramble moderate
destinations.highlights: Winter hammerhead schools at Yonaguni, summer mantas at Ishigaki

destinations.name: Cocos Island
destinations.currents: Strong (1-4kt) + surge; advanced & liveaboard-only (~36h crossing); reef-hook hanging at Bajo Alcyone; Dirty Rock sheltered; currents strengthen wet season (Jun-Nov upwelling)
destinations.highlights: Hammerhead-school mecca; remote 36h crossing, strong currents/thermoclines.

Jan.marine_life: Tiger sharks; mobula schools; silky sharks; hammerheads (steady)
Jan.conditions: Dry: viz >30m, calm crossings; thermocline to ~22C

Feb.marine_life: Tiger sharks; mobula schools; silkies; Galapagos/whitetip sharks

May.marine_life: Mobula schools tail; whale sharks (plankton rising)

Jun.conditions: Wet begins; currents strengthen; viz dropping

Sep.conditions: Wet peak; rough; low viz but peak action

Oct.conditions: Wet peak; rough crossings; green water

Dec.marine_life: Tiger sharks; mobula schools building; silkies

destinations.name: Galapagos Islands
destinations.currents: Notoriously strong - 4 converging currents; negative entries, down-currents, surge; Darwin & Wolf advanced/liveaboard-only; Gordon's Rocks 'washing machine'
destinations.highlights: Big-animal mecca; whale sharks & hammerhead schools at Darwin/Wolf.

Jan.marine_life: Mantas; hammerhead schools (smaller); sea lions, iguanas
Jan.conditions: Warm season; calm, smooth crossings; viz 15-30m

Jun.marine_life: Whale sharks arriving; hammerheads building; penguins active
Jun.conditions: Cool season begins; choppier; viz dropping

Aug.marine_life: Whale shark + hammerhead PEAK (Darwin/Wolf); mola mola
Aug.conditions: Coldest begins; cold thermoclines; rough; viz 5-20m

Sep.marine_life: Peak megafauna: whale sharks, hammerheads, mola
Sep.conditions: Coldest; rough; low viz but peak animals

Oct.marine_life: Whale sharks tapering (still peak); hammerheads; mola

destinations.name: Socorro Island
destinations.currents: Open-ocean surge and currents around seamounts; advanced conditions
destinations.highlights: Liveaboard-only Revillagigedo with friendly giant oceanic mantas

destinations.name: Sea of Cortez
destinations.currents: Variable; seamounts like El Bajo can have strong currents
destinations.highlights: Los Islotes sea lions, mobula swarms and La Paz whale sharks

Apr.marine_life: Mobula rays, whale sharks, sea lions

Oct.marine_life: Sea lions, schooling fish, whale sharks

Nov.conditions: Warm, great viz, whale sharks return

Dec.conditions: Cooling, good viz, whale shark season

destinations.name: Maui & Kona
destinations.currents: Mostly calm leeward Kona; Maui Molokini mild to moderate
destinations.highlights: Kona manta-ray night dives year-round plus Molokini crater

destinations.name: Chagos Archipelago / BIOT
destinations.currents: Strong oceanic currents on exposed atolls; drift diving common
destinations.highlights: Ultra-remote pristine MPA atolls, permit/expedition-only, abundant sharks and untouched coral

destinations.name: Maldives
destinations.currents: Monsoon-driven channel (kandu) drift; NE monsoon (Dec-Apr) dive east side for viz/pelagics, mantas west; SW monsoon (May-Nov) dive west side, mantas/whale sharks east; strongest at full/new moon
destinations.highlights: Monsoon-driven channel drifts; Hanifaru manta aggregation Jul-Oct.

Jan.marine_life: Hammerheads Rasdhoo (dawn); Fuvahmulah tiger sharks; grey reef sharks

Apr.marine_life: Hammerhead season closing; manta activity strengthening

May.marine_life: Manta peak begins; whale sharks building
May.conditions: Transition; SW monsoon starts; viz dropping

Jul.marine_life: Whale shark peak (S Ari); Hanifaru manta aggregation begins

Aug.conditions: Best Hanifaru activity; viz 10-25m; full/new moon best

Sep.conditions: Hanifaru peak; lower viz; plankton soup

Oct.marine_life: Hanifaru tail; whale sharks frequent; mantas

Dec.marine_life: Hammerheads Rasdhoo returning; Fuvahmulah tigers; grey reef sharks
Dec.conditions: NE monsoon: best viz returns 30-40m, calm

destinations.name: Seychelles
destinations.currents: Inner islands (Mahe/Praslin) mild & beginner-friendly; SE trades May-Oct bring 20-30kt winds, surge, upwelling; outer islands/Aldabra liveaboard/permit only
destinations.highlights: Granite-boulder reefs; whale shark season Aug-Nov; calm transition-month diving.

Jan.marine_life: Hawksbill turtle nesting peak; eagle rays; whale sharks may linger
Jan.conditions: NW monsoon; warm, sheltered SE coasts

Feb.marine_life: Turtle nesting; reef life, eagle rays

Mar.marine_life: Turtle nesting; early whale sharks (Amirantes late Mar)

May.marine_life: Whale shark peak (Amirantes); moving to inner waters

Jun.marine_life: Whale sharks through inner islands; trevally/jack schools
Jun.conditions: SE trades established; choppy exposed coasts

Jul.marine_life: Whale sharks; pelagic action outer chains

Aug.marine_life: Whale shark aggregation building (Mahe NW/Beau Vallon); spinner dolphins

Sep.marine_life: Whale shark season opens in earnest; plankton blooms

Oct.marine_life: Whale shark PEAK; manta activity rising

Nov.marine_life: Whale shark best month; manta peak

Dec.marine_life: Whale sharks tailing off; hawksbill nesting begins

destinations.name: Aliwal Shoal
destinations.currents: Often strong; surf launch by RIB; variable drift conditions
destinations.highlights: Sardine run bait balls plus raggie and baited tiger shark dives

Jun.conditions: Cool water, bait balls, viz variable

destinations.name: Ningaloo Reef
destinations.currents: Sheltered lagoon mild; outer reef and drift sites moderate
destinations.highlights: Whale shark snorkeling on the reef plus year-round mantas and turtles

destinations.name: Gozo & Malta
destinations.currents: Mostly mild; some exposed sites affected by swell
destinations.highlights: Clear-water wrecks, caverns and famous Gozo shore dives

destinations.name: Medes Islands
destinations.currents: Generally mild; sheltered marine reserve, occasional surface current
destinations.highlights: Costa Brava marine reserve famed for huge friendly groupers

destinations.name: Palau
destinations.currents: Tidal, strongest at full/new moon; Blue Corner (reef hooks, viz 28m+ incoming), Ulong Channel (high-speed drift), German Channel (gentle, manta cleaning), Peleliu (dangerous down-currents, dry-season only)
destinations.highlights: Blue Corner drift & reef-hook diving; lunar spawning aggregations.

Jan.marine_life: Moorish idol aggregation (full moon); mantas; grey reef sharks; whale sharks
Jan.conditions: Dry season; calm; viz >30m; Jellyfish Lake

Feb.marine_life: Snapper & moorish idol spawning (full moon); mantas; grey reef sharks

Mar.marine_life: Grey-reef shark peak begins; bumphead spawning (new moon); mantas

Apr.marine_life: Grey-reef shark peak; bumphead spawning; mantas; whale sharks

May.marine_life: Grey-reef shark peak; mantas feeding on plankton

Jun.marine_life: Camouflage grouper spawn (Jun-Aug new moon); mantas; sharks

Aug.conditions: Wet; strong W winds; viz reduced

destinations.name: Truk (Chuuk) Lagoon
destinations.currents: Sheltered lagoon - mild to negligible; mild current only on some wrecks/passes; far calmer than open ocean; near-uniform temp surface-to-depth (no thermocline)
destinations.highlights: World's premier WWII wreck dive; 50+ ships in a calm lagoon.

Jan.marine_life: Coral-encrusted wrecks (266+ fish sp); reef sharks, turtles, rays

Feb.marine_life: Wreck reefs; reef sharks (deep wrecks); turtles
Feb.conditions: Drier; calm; coolest but ~28C; best viz

Mar.marine_life: Wreck marine life; sharks, rays, turtles
Mar.conditions: Drier season; calm seas; good viz

Apr.marine_life: Wreck reefs; sharks, turtles, occasional manta
Apr.conditions: Dry sweet spot; calm; viz good

May.marine_life: Wreck marine life; reef fish schools
May.conditions: Transition to wet; wettest month; choppier

Nov.conditions: Among warmest; viz best when not raining

Dec.conditions: Drier season begins; calm; viz improving

destinations.name: Cenotes of Yucatán Peninsula
destinations.currents: Virtually none; still freshwater with haloclines in caverns and caves
destinations.highlights: Crystal-clear freshwater caverns with stalactites, haloclines and dramatic light beams

destinations.name: Azores
destinations.currents: Offshore seamounts have strong currents; pelagic drift dives
destinations.highlights: Atlantic pelagic diving - blue/mako sharks and mantas at seamounts

destinations.name: Lundy Island
destinations.currents: Strong tidal currents; slack-water diving required
destinations.highlights: UK's first marine reserve - playful grey seal encounters

destinations.name: Scapa Flow
destinations.currents: Sheltered anchorage but tidal; channels need slack water
destinations.highlights: WWI German High Seas Fleet - world-class wreck diving

destinations.name: Silfra Fissure
destinations.currents: Negligible gentle drift through fissure; near still water
destinations.highlights: Crystal 100m+ viz freshwater dive between tectonic plates

Jan.marine_life: Crystal viz, no marine life - geology dive

Feb.marine_life: Crystal viz, no marine life - geology dive

Mar.marine_life: Crystal viz, no marine life - geology dive

Apr.marine_life: Crystal viz, no marine life - geology dive

May.marine_life: Crystal viz, no marine life - geology dive

Jun.marine_life: Crystal viz, no marine life - geology dive

Jul.marine_life: Crystal viz, no marine life - geology dive

Aug.marine_life: Crystal viz, no marine life - geology dive

Sep.marine_life: Crystal viz, no marine life - geology dive

Oct.marine_life: Crystal viz, no marine life - geology dive

Nov.marine_life: Crystal viz, no marine life - geology dive

Dec.marine_life: Crystal viz, no marine life - geology dive

destinations.name: Vancouver Island
destinations.currents: Strong tidal currents; slack-water diving in passes
destinations.highlights: Cold-water Pacific - wolf eels, giant octopus, sea lions

Feb.marine_life: Giant octopus, wolf eels, sea lions

Oct.marine_life: Wolf eels, octopus, sea lions arrive

Dec.marine_life: Giant octopus, wolf eels, sea lions

destinations.name: Red Sea (Egypt)
destinations.currents: Sheltered bays mild; offshore marine parks (Brothers, Daedalus, Elphinstone) strong unpredictable drift; winter N winds can cancel exposed sites
destinations.highlights: Year-round reefs & wrecks; offshore shark reefs (Brothers/Daedalus/Elphinstone).

Jan.marine_life: Oceanic whitetip (south), threshers possible; reef & wreck focus
Jan.conditions: Clearest 'crystal' viz 20-40m; cool, occasional N wind

Mar.marine_life: Big Fish season starts; mantas, early whale sharks late month

Apr.marine_life: Big Fish season; mantas, early whale sharks

May.marine_life: Whale sharks building; hammerheads ramping at offshore reefs

Jun.marine_life: Whale shark peak begins; hammerheads building

Jul.conditions: Hottest air; calmest crossings; slight plankton dip

Dec.conditions: Mild winter; clear viz; occasional wind

destinations.name: Fiji
destinations.currents: Strong tidal currents in Somosomo Strait feed soft corals
destinations.highlights: Soft-coral capital with Beqa Lagoon bull-shark dives

destinations.name: French Polynesia
destinations.currents: Strong drift in Tiputa (Rangiroa) and Fakarava passes, tide-dependent
destinations.highlights: Atoll passes with grey-reef shark walls and big-fish drifts

destinations.name: Guadalcanal & Western Province
destinations.currents: Generally mild reef currents; some channels and passages drift
destinations.highlights: WWII wrecks and pristine reefs with rich macro life

destinations.name: Moorea
destinations.currents: Generally mild lagoon and outer-reef currents, some channel flow
destinations.highlights: Lemon-shark dives and seasonal humpback whale encounters

destinations.name: Poor Knights Islands
destinations.currents: Moderate; some current at exposed points and pinnacles
destinations.highlights: Cousteau-praised reserve - arches, caves, subtropical fish schools

destinations.name: Apo Island & Dumaguete
destinations.currents: Mild to moderate at Apo walls; calm sandy muck sites near Dumaguete
destinations.highlights: Sea turtles on Apo's reefs plus world-class muck diving at Dauin

destinations.name: Bunaken National Marine Park
destinations.currents: Mild to moderate wall currents; occasional drift along drop-offs
destinations.highlights: Sheer Sulawesi walls teeming with turtles and reef fish, superb visibility

destinations.name: Gili Islands & Lombok
destinations.currents: Mild on most sites; can be moderate to strong on channel and drift sites
destinations.highlights: Reliable year-round turtles over easy reefs, ideal for beginners

destinations.name: Malapascua Island
destinations.currents: Moderate currents at Monad Shoal; some sites can be strong
destinations.highlights: Year-round dawn thresher sharks at Monad Shoal plus rich macro life

destinations.name: Similan Islands
destinations.currents: Mild to moderate; can be strong on exposed pinnacles and channels
destinations.highlights: Granite boulders, mantas and whale sharks in the Andaman Sea, liveaboard season only

destinations.name: Tubbataha Reefs Natural Park
destinations.currents: Strong oceanic currents; drift diving common on the walls
destinations.highlights: UNESCO atoll with sharks, mantas and pristine walls, liveaboard-only short season

destinations.name: Lord Howe Island
destinations.currents: Protected lagoon plus exposed sites with moderate current/surge
destinations.highlights: World's southernmost coral reef blending tropical and temperate species

destinations.name: South West Rocks
destinations.currents: Fish Rock Cave can have strong tidal currents and surge
destinations.highlights: Fish Rock Cave swim-through with grey nurse shark aggregations

Jan.conditions: Warmest, EAC viz, can be strong current
